title: cotula pectinata subsp. villosa
intro: Cotula pectinata subsp. villosa, also known as the hairy cotula or hairy cotula daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cotula pectinata subsp. villosa include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy in traditional medicine. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for various health issues, and its dried leaves and flowers are often used in herbal preparations.
